devanagari: विदुर उवाच । स्वायम्भुवस्य च मनोः वंशः परमसम्मतः । कथ्यतां भगवन्यत्र मैथुनेनैधिरे प्रजाः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvāca svāyambhuvasya ca manoḥ vaṃśaḥ paramasammataḥ kathyatāṃ bhagavanyatra maithunenaidhire prajāḥ

translation:
Vidura said:
Oh venerable sage, (I pray you) to please describe to me the greatly respected dynasty of Svāyambhuva Manu in which descendants multiplied in marital relations.

devanagari: प्रियव्रतोत्तानपादौ सुतौ स्वायम्भुवस्य वै । यथाधर्मं जुगुपतुः सप्तद्वीपवतीं महीम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: priyavratottānapādau sutau svāyambhuvasya vai yathādharmaṃ jugupatuḥ saptadvīpavatīṃ mahīm

translation: Svāyambhuva Manu had two sons, Priyavrata and Uttānapāda, who protected the earth consisting of seven insular-continents[1] according to religion.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
According to Purāṇic geography the earth consists of seven insular continents, viz., Jambū, Plakṣa, Śālmali, Kuśa, Krauñca, Śāka and Puṣkara (N.L. De—GDAMI p. 178). V.S. Agrawala and D.C. Sircar point out that the Purāṇic concept of seven concentric island-continents is a later development. The original concept was of caturdvīpā Vasumatī, i.e. the earth was like a lotus with Mt. Meru as its Karṇikā (pericarp) and the following island-continents as its petals in the four directions of Meru: (1) Kuru or Uttara Kuru in the north, (2) Jambū or Bhārata in the south, (3) Bhadrāśva in the east and (4) Ketumāla in the west. These have been tentatively identified with (1) northern portion of Asia, north of the Altai mountain, (2) India, (3) China and east Asia and (4) the valley of the Oxus and west Asia. Ancient Buddhist texts support the concept of caturdvīpā Vasumatī.—For details see V.S. Agrawala—MP—A Study, pp. 184- 188. Sircar—SGAMI, pp. 17-26. Baldeva Upadhyaya Purāṇa Vimarśa, PP. 317-330.

devanagari: तस्य वै दुहिता ब्रह्मन् देवहूतीति विश्रुता । पत्‍नी प्रजापतेरुक्ता कर्दमस्य त्वयानघ ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya vai duhitā brahman devahūtīti viśrutā pat‍nī prajāpateruktā kardamasya tvayānagha

translation: He had a daughter well known as Devahūti. Oh sinless one, you told that she was the wife of Prajāpati Kardama.

devanagari: तस्यां स वै महायोगी युक्तायां योगलक्षणैः । ससर्ज कतिधा वीर्यं तन्मे शुश्रूषवे वद ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ sa vai mahāyogī yuktāyāṃ yogalakṣaṇaiḥ sasarja katidhā vīryaṃ tanme śuśrūṣave vada

translation: Please tell me who am desirous of hearing, how many children had the great Yogī Kardama, got from her who possessed śama, dama and other qualities of Yoga.

devanagari: रुचिर्यो भगवान् ब्रह्मन् दक्षो वा ब्रह्मणः सुतः । यथा ससर्ज भूतानि लब्ध्वा भार्यां च मानवीम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ruciryo bhagavān brahman dakṣo vā brahmaṇaḥ sutaḥ yathā sasarja bhūtāni labdhvā bhāryāṃ ca mānavīm

translation: How did respectable Ruci or Dakṣa, the sons of God Brahmā get the daughters of Manu as wives (Ruci marrying Ākūti and Dakṣa marrying Prasūti) and procreated children, Oh Brahmaṇa [Brāhmaṇa?]?

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । प्रजाः सृजेति भगवान् कर्दमो ब्रह्मणोदितः । सरस्वत्यां तपस्तेपे सहस्राणां समा दश ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca prajāḥ sṛjeti bhagavān kardamo brahmaṇoditaḥ sarasvatyāṃ tapastepe sahasrāṇāṃ samā daśa

translation:
Maitreya said:
When the venerable sage Kardama was commanded by God Brahmā to create beings, he performed penance for ten thousand years on the bank of the Sarasvatī.

devanagari: ततः समाधियुक्तेन क्रियायोगेन कर्दमः । सम्प्रपेदे हरिं भक्त्या प्रपन्नवरदाशुषम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ samādhiyuktena kriyāyogena kardamaḥ samprapede hariṃ bhaktyā prapannavaradāśuṣam

translation: Then Kardama with great devotion, intense meditation and worship[2], rendered service to Hari who gives boons to those who resort to him.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
kriyā-yoga—(i) Following the prescribed religious duties according to his varṇa and āśrama without selfish motive.—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Service of the Lord who manifests himself while in meditation.—Subodhinī
(iii) Acts of worship prescribed in the Vedas and the Tantras.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: तावत्प्रसन्नो भगवान् पुष्कराक्षः कृते युगे । दर्शयामास तं क्षत्तः शाब्दं ब्रह्म दधद्वपुः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvatprasanno bhagavān puṣkarākṣaḥ kṛte yuge darśayāmāsa taṃ kṣattaḥ śābdaṃ brahma dadhadvapuḥ

translation: Then the lotus-eyed God Viṣṇu became pleased with him. Oh Vidura, in the Kṛta age, he manifested himself in an auspicious form, though he is known (to the world) by the verbal description in the Vedas[3].
purport:
Footnote [3]:
śābdaṃ brahma—(i) Form known only from the description in the five Upaniṣaḍs and possessing all excellences—Bhāgavata Candrikā (ii) Full of all excellent attributes to be known from Vedic texts.—Padaratnāvalī (iii) Manifesting form full of sat, cit and ānanda—VC.

english_devnagari: sa taṃ virajamarkābhaṃ sitapadmotpalasrajam snigdhanīlālakavrāta vaktrābjaṃ virajo

translation: In the sky, he saw the Lord who was free from all impurities and was resplendent like the Sun. He wore a garland of white lotuses that blossom by day and night. His lotuslike face was beautiful with smooth blue-black locks of hair. He was clad in pure silk garment. The Lord wore a crown and ear-rings and held in his hands a conch, a disc, a mace and a white lotus for sport. His captivating smiles and looks delighted the heart. His lotus-like feet were placed on the shoulders of Garuḍa. He had Lakṣmī (Śrī-Vatsa) on his bosom and the Kaustubha gem round His neck.

devanagari: किरीटिनं कुण्डलिनं शङ्खचक्रगदाधरम् । श्वेतोत्पलक्रीडनकं मनःस्पर्शस्मितेक्षणम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: kirīṭinaṃ kuṇḍalinaṃ śaṅkhacakragadādharam śvetotpalakrīḍanakaṃ manaḥsparśasmitekṣaṇam

translation: In the sky, he saw the Lord who was free from all impurities and was resplendent like the Sun. He wore a garland of white lotuses that blossom by day and night. His lotuslike face was beautiful with smooth blue-black locks of hair. He was clad in pure silk garment. The Lord wore a crown and ear-rings and held in his hands a conch, a disc, a mace and a white lotus for sport. His captivating smiles and looks delighted the heart. translation: In the sky, he saw the Lord who was free from all impurities and was resplendent like the Sun. He wore a garland of white lotuses that blossom by day and night. His lotuslike face was beautiful with smooth blue-black locks of hair. He was clad in pure silk garment. The Lord wore a crown and ear-rings and held in his hands a conch, a disc, a mace and a white lotus for sport. His captivating smiles and looks delighted the heart. His lotus-like feet were placed on the shoulders of Garuḍa. He had Lakṣmī (Śrī-Vatsa) on his bosom and the Kaustubha gem round His neck.

devanagari: जातहर्षोऽपतन्मूर्ध्ना क्षितौ लब्धमनोरथः । गीर्भिस्त्वभ्यगृणात्प्रीति स्वभावात्मा कृताञ्जलिः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: jātaharṣo gīrbhistvabhyagṛṇātprīti svabhāvātmā kṛtāñjaliḥ

translation: Overjoyed at the fulfilment of his desired object, Kardama prostrated himself on the ground. He, who was intensely devoted by nature, folded his hands and praised the Lord (in the following words).

devanagari: ऋषिरुवाच । जुष्टं बताद्याखिलसत्त्वराशेः । सांसिध्यमक्ष्णोस्तव दर्शनान्नः । यद्दर्शनं जन्मभिरीड्य सद्भिः । आशासते योगिनो रूढयोगाः ॥ १३ ॥

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
The sage said:
Oh praiseworthy Lord, what a joy it is! Real usefulness of our eyes has been achieved today by visualising you whose entire personality consists of perfectly pure Sattva[4]—a sight coveted by Yogins who have been developing their Yoga through many progressively pious births.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
sattva-rāśi [rāśiḥ]—Reservoir of all that is good and powerful—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: ये मायया ते हतमेधसस्त्वत् । पादारविन्दं भवसिन्धुपोतम् । उपासते कामलवाय तेषां । रासीश कामान् निरयेऽपि ये स्युः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ye māyayā te hatamedhasastvat pādāravindaṃ bhavasindhupotam upāsate kāmalavāya teṣāṃ rāsīśa kāmān niraye
translation: Your lotus-like feet are like a boat to cross the ocean of Saṃsāra. But those whose intelligence is deadened by your Māyā, resort to them for petty pleasures which are available even in hell. But, Oh Lord, you fulfil even those trivial desires.

devanagari: तथा स चाहं परिवोढुकामः । समानशीलां गृहमेधधेनुम् । उपेयिवान् मूलमशेषमूलं । दुराशयः कामदुघाङ्घ्रिपस्य ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā sa cāhaṃ parivoḍhukāmaḥ samānaśīlāṃ gṛhamedhadhenum upeyivān mūlamaśeṣamūlaṃ durāśayaḥ kāmadughāṅghripasya
translation: I am of such a nature (as described above). I wish to marry a girl similar to me in disposition and useful like a cow to a householder’s life (in yielding three objectives in life, namely, dharma, artha and kāma). With this unbecoming motive, I approached your feet which are like a wish-yielding tree and which are the source of all (four puruṣārthas).

devanagari: प्रजापतेस्ते वचसाधीश तन्त्या । लोकः किलायं कामहतोऽनुबद्धः । अहं च लोकानुगतो वहामि । बलिं च शुक्लानिमिषाय तुभ्यम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: prajāpateste vacasādhīśa tantyā lokaḥ kilāyaṃ kāmahato ahaṃ ca lokānugato vahāmi baliṃ ca śuklānimiṣāya tubhyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Oh Supreme Lord, this world[5] is overwhelmed with desire. It is really bound down by the cord in the form of words (Vedic injunctions) expressed by you[6], the lord of Prajās. Oh embodiment of pure[7] Dharma, I am verily a follower of the world. I carry offerings to you (i.e. abide by your order of performing the prescribed kārmic duties for repaying the three traditional debts (ṛṇa) of man for which wife is essential)—you are the soul of Time.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
loka—Marīci and others—VC.

Footnote [6]:
te—(i) It is at your behest that Marīci and others procreate. It is not their fault—VC.
(ii) Your son Brahmā, the creator—Subodhinī

Footnote [7]:
śukla—(i) One who destroys the misery of Saṃsāra and bestows higher bliss—Padaratnāvalī
(ii) This adjective suggests that there will not be any misery from Saṃsāra—Subodhinī

devanagari: लोकांश्च लोकानुगतान् पशूंश्च । हित्वा श्रितास्ते चरणातपत्रम् । परस्परं त्वद्‍गुणवादसीधु । पीयूषनिर्यापितदेहधर्माः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: lokāṃśca lokānugatān paśūṃśca hitvā śritāste caraṇātapatram parasparaṃ tvad‍guṇavādasīdhu pīyūṣaniryāpitadehadharmāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śri class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śri class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ śri class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śri class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ śri class 1 verb]

translation: Having abandoned worldly men and their followers like beasts[8], (your devotees) resort to the umbrella in the form of your feet. They forget the conditions, i.e. the needs of their bodies (such as hunger, thirst, etc.) in the discussion of Your attributes—a discussion which is intoxicating like wine (making them forget their worldly ties) and sweet like nectar.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
VC. regards that both the learned ones in Śāstras and their followers, being devoid of devotion (bhakti) are like beasts. Hence.no sin in committed in abandoning them.

devanagari: न तेऽजराक्षभ्रमिरायुरेषां । त्रयोदशारं त्रिशतं षष्टिपर्व । षण्नेम्यनन्तच्छदि यत्त्रिणाभि । करालस्रोतो जगदाच्छिद्य धावत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: na te trayodaśāraṃ triśataṃ ṣaṣṭiparva ṣaṇnemyanantacchadi yattriṇābhi karālasroto jagadācchidya dhāvat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Your wheel of Time which is based on three supports and which attracts and affects the world and has terrific speed, does not erode the life of your devotee while it moves on—This wheel of Time rotates round the axis of eternal Brahman. It has thirteen spokes (twelve months plus one additional i.e. adhika month). It has three hundred and sixty joints (number of days in the year). It has six tyres (i.e. seasons), innumerable blades (such as small units of time like kṣaṇa, nimiṣa etc.), three supports, namely, three periods of four months—caturmāsa each.

devanagari: एकः स्वयं सन्जगतः सिसृक्षया । अद्वितीययात्मन् अधि योगमायया । सृजस्यदः पासि पुनर्ग्रसिष्यसे । यथोर्णनाभिः भगवन् स्वशक्तिभिः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ekaḥ svayaṃ sanjagataḥ sisṛkṣayā advitīyayātman adhi yogamāyayā sṛjasyadaḥ pāsi punargrasiṣyase yathorṇanābhiḥ bhagavan svaśaktibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Oh Lord, you are only one, i.e. there is none other except you. With the desire of creating the world and by the powers such as Sattva etc., assumed by you through your Yoga- Māyā, you create, protect and destroy this world like a spider (doing with his web).

devanagari: नैतद्‍बताधीश पदं तवेप्सितं । यन्मायया नस्तनुषे भूतसूक्ष्मम् । अनुग्रहायास्त्वपि यर्हि मायया । लसत्तुलस्या तनुवा विलक्षितः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: naitad‍batādhīśa padaṃ tavepsitaṃ yanmāyayā nastanuṣe bhūtasūkṣmam anugrahāyāstvapi yarhi māyayā lasattulasyā tanuvā vilakṣitaḥ

[nominative single from √ las class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ las class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ las class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Supreme Lord, although you extend (offer) by means of your Māyā, worldly pleasures in the form of objects of senses to us, your devotees, this is not really your desired object. Still, let it be offered out of your grace towards us; for you have manifested yourself as a person decorated with resplendent Tulasī (and hence have Time-Space limitation) through Māyā. (This manifestation will lead to worldly pleasures here and Liberation hereafter)[9].
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Most of the commentators follow Bhāvāratha Dīpikā A few different interpretations are noted below:
(i) Oh Lord, by your Māyā you create ahaṃkāra, subtle elements and their special characteristics. Or you create ego whereby we cease to aspire after your place (pada). But I feel that despite my nonliking for your place, you will bless me with it out of your grace. As you have manifested yourself in your miraculous person with Tulasī garland, I believe it is for the grace of attaining your place.—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Oh Lord, you have adopted the Brahmāṇḍa or Bhūtasūkṣma by your own will (Māyā) without being impelled by any one else. But this is not your essential form described in the Upaniṣads. The Lord is visualised by me as decorated with Tulasī garland, ear-rings etc. This is for showing grace to the devotees.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: तं त्वानुभूत्योपरतक्रियार्थं । स्वमायया वर्तितलोकतन्त्रम् । नमाम्यभीक्ष्णं नमनीयपाद । सरोजमल्पीयसि कामवर्षम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tvānubhūtyoparatakriyārthaṃ svamāyayā vartitalokatantram namāmyabhīkṣṇaṃ namanīyapāda sarojamalpīyasi kāmavarṣam
translation: I bow to you again and again. By your Supreme knowledge, you are free from the experience of the fruit of Karmas. You manage the working of the universe by your Māyā. Hence your lotus-like feet are worthy of being bowed (by devotees, whether they cherish desire or not). You shower desired objects upon a devotee who is motivated (even) by trivial desires.

devanagari: ऋषिरुवाच । इत्यव्यलीकं प्रणुतोऽब्जनाभः । तं आबभाषे वचसामृतेन । सुपर्णपक्षोपरि रोचमानः । प्रेमस्मित उद्वीक्षणविभ्रमद्‍भ्रूः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣiruvāca ityavyalīkaṃ praṇuto taṃ ābabhāṣe vacasāmṛtena suparṇapakṣopari rocamānaḥ premasmita udvīkṣaṇavibhramad‍bhrūḥ

translation:
The sage said:
Being thus praised sincerely, Lord Viṣṇu, the God with a lotus in His navel, who appeared lustrous (in his seat) above the wings of Garuḍa and whose eyebrows moved gracefully by his gracious looks and his affectionate smile, spoke to Kardama in nectar-like sweet words.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । विदित्वा तव चैत्यं मे पुरैव समयोजि तत् । यदर्थं आत्मनियमैः त्वयैवाहं समर्चितः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca viditvā tava caityaṃ me puraiva samayoji tat yadarthaṃ ātmaniyamaiḥ tvayaivāhaṃ samarcitaḥ

translation:
The Lord said:
Having known your intention, I have already arranged for that very object for which I have been properly worshipped by you, with self-imposed religious observations.

devanagari: न वै जातु मृषैव स्यात् प्रजाध्यक्ष मदर्हणम् । भवद्विधेष्वतितरां मयि सङ्गृभितात्मनाम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: na vai jātu mṛṣaiva syāt prajādhyakṣa madarhaṇam bhavadvidheṣvatitarāṃ mayi saṅgṛbhitātmanām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Oh Kardama (Lord of Prajās) I My worship performed by persons with their minds concentrated in me is never futile. In case of persons like you, it is not all fruitless.

devanagari: प्रजापतिसुतः सम्राट् मनुर्विख्यातमङ्गलः । ब्रह्मावर्तं योऽधिवसन् शास्ति सप्तार्णवां महीम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: prajāpatisutaḥ samrāṭ manurvikhyātamaṅgalaḥ brahmāvartaṃ yo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vas class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vas class 1 verb]

translation: Manu, king of kings, the son of Prajāpati (Brahmā) is well known for his prosperity and righteous conduct. He lives in Brahmāvarta, but rules the whole earth surrounded by seven oceans[10].
purport:
Footnote [10]:
Saptārṇava—N.C. De identifies the seven oceans surrounding the earth as follows: (1) Lavaṇa (—the Indian ocean surrounding India—Jambūdvīpa), (2) Kṣīra (Kṣīra is a hyper Sanskritisation of ‘Shirwan’, i.e. the Caspian sea to the north of Śāka dvīpa), (3) Surā (Sanskritisation of the sea of Sarain, i.e. the Caspian sea forming the Southern or South-eastern boundary of Kuśadvīpā), (4) Ghṛta—the Erythraean sea or the Persian gulf, (5) Ikṣu—another name of the river Oxus—the big river taken as a sea, (6) Dadhi—a Sanskritisation of Dahae—the Scythic tribe living on the shore of the sea of Aral. Name of the people transferred to the sea, (7) Svādu—Sanskritisation of Tchadun, a river in Mongolia flowing through Plakṣadvīpa.—N.L. De—GDAMI, p. 179.

devanagari: स चेह विप्र राजर्षिः महिष्या शतरूपया । आयास्यति दिदृक्षुस्त्वां परश्वो धर्मकोविदः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ceha vipra rājarṣiḥ mahiṣyā śatarūpayā āyāsyati didṛkṣustvāṃ paraśvo dharmakovidaḥ
translation: The sage-king who is well-versed in Dharma will come here day after tomorrow, along with Queen Śatarūpā with the object of seeing you.

devanagari: आत्मजां असितापाङ्गीं वयःशीलगुणान्विताम् । मृगयन्तीं पतिं दास्यति अनुरूपाय ते प्रभो ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmajāṃ asitāpāṅgīṃ vayaḥśīlaguṇānvitām mṛgayantīṃ patiṃ dāsyati anurūpāya te prabho

translation: Oh Kardama, he will offer to you as a worthy bridegroom his daughter of dark eyes, of proper age, character and qualities and of marriageable age.

devanagari: समाहितं ते हृदयं यत्र इमान् परिवत्सरान् । सा त्वां ब्रह्मन् नृपवधूः काममाशु भजिष्यति ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: samāhitaṃ te hṛdayaṃ yatra imān parivatsarān sā tvāṃ brahman nṛpavadhūḥ kāmamāśu bhajiṣyati
translation: Oh Brāhmaṇa, that princess, of herself, will willingly resort to you (as your wife) in this place where you have spent years in meditation occupied with the desire of having a suitable wife.

devanagari: या ते आत्मभृतं वीर्यं नवधा प्रसविष्यति । वीर्ये त्वदीये ऋषय आधास्यन्ति अञ्जसात्मनः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yā te ātmabhṛtaṃ vīryaṃ navadhā prasaviṣyati vīrye tvadīye ṛṣaya ādhāsyanti añjasātmanaḥ

translation: From you, she will give birth to nine children (lit. she will nine times give birth to your semen borne by her) and sages will soon beget children from your daughters.

devanagari: त्वं च सम्यग् अनुष्ठाय निदेशं मे उशत्तमः । मयि तीर्थीकृताशेष क्रियार्थो मां प्रपत्स्यसे ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ ca samyag anuṣṭhāya nideśaṃ me uśattamaḥ mayi tīrthīkṛtāśeṣa kriyārtho māṃ prapatsyase

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vaś class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vaś class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vaś class 2 verb]

translation: Having carried out properly my command (for procreation) you, of pure mind, will offer upto me all the fruits of your action and finally attain unto me.

devanagari: कृत्वा दयां च जीवेषु दत्त्वा चाभयमात्मवान् । मय्यात्मानं सह जगद् द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मनि चापि माम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtvā dayāṃ ca jīveṣu dattvā cābhayamātmavān mayyātmānaṃ saha jagad drakṣyasyātmani cāpi mām
translation: Having conferred mercy (on the needy, in the householder’s stage of life) and having offered protection from fear (as a saṃnyāsin—recluse) and being self-controlled, you will realize yourself and the world in me and myself in yourself.

devanagari: सहाहं स्वांशकलया त्वद्वीर्येण महामुने । तव क्षेत्रे देवहूत्यां प्रणेष्ये तत्त्वसंहिताम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: sahāhaṃ svāṃśakalayā tvadvīryeṇa mahāmune tava kṣetre devahūtyāṃ praṇeṣye tattvasaṃhitām

translation: Oh great sage, a portion of mine will be born of you as a son from your wife Devahūti. I shall compose a treatise of ultimate truths (the Sāṃkhya Śāstra).

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एवं तं अनुभाष्याथ भगवान् प्रत्यगक्षजः । जगाम बिन्दुसरसः सरस्वत्या परिश्रितात् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca evaṃ taṃ anubhāṣyātha bhagavān pratyagakṣajaḥ jagāma bindusarasaḥ sarasvatyā pariśritāt

translation:
Maitreya said:
Having addressed thus, the Lord who manifests himself to senses which are turned inwards, then departed from Bindusaras surrounded by Sarasvatī.

devanagari: निरीक्षतस्तस्य ययावशेष । सिद्धेश्वराभिष्टुतसिद्धमार्गः । आकर्णयन् पत्ररथेन्द्रपक्षैः । उच्चारितं स्तोममुदीर्णसाम ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: nirīkṣatastasya yayāvaśeṣa siddheśvarābhiṣṭutasiddhamārgaḥ ākarṇayan patrarathendrapakṣaiḥ uccāritaṃ stomamudīrṇasāma

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: While Kardama was looking on, he (Viṣṇu) who had been praised by all prominent Siddhas and was sought after by (all) Siddhas[11] went away, hearing the collection of Stoma hymns sung in Sāma notes, as a result of (the flutterings of) Garuḍa’s wings.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Siddha-mārga—Alternatively: the path to Vaikuṇṭha—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā Dīpinī doubts whether Siddha means Vaikuṇṭha. Bhāgavata Candrikā, Subodhinī derive it: Path that is self-established. Siddhāntapradīpa: The path of knowledge and devotion that has been established.

devanagari: अथ सम्प्रस्थिते शुक्ले कर्दमो भगवान् ऋषिः । आस्ते स्म बिन्दुसरसि तं कालं प्रतिपालयन् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: atha samprasthite śukle kardamo bhagavān ṛṣiḥ āste sma bindusarasi taṃ kālaṃ pratipālayan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: On the departure of the pure lustrous God Viṣṇu, the venerable sage Kardama stayed at Bindusaras waiting for the time (of Manu’s arrival).

devanagari: मनुः स्यन्दनमास्थाय शातकौम्भपरिच्छदम् । आरोप्य स्वां दुहितरं सभार्यः पर्यटन् महीम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: manuḥ syandanamāsthāya śātakaumbhaparicchadam āropya svāṃ duhitaraṃ sabhāryaḥ paryaṭan mahīm
translation: Manu got into his chariot decorated with plates of gold. Along with his wife and daughter he drove over the world.

devanagari: तस्मिन् सुधन्वन्नहनि भगवान् यत्समादिशत् । उपायादाश्रमपदं मुनेः शान्तव्रतस्य तत् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin sudhanvannahani bhagavān yatsamādiśat upāyādāśramapadaṃ muneḥ śāntavratasya tat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh good archer Vidura, on the day which was appointed by Lord Viṣṇu, he arrived at the hermitage of the sage who had completed the vow of celibacy.

devanagari: यस्मिन्भगवतो नेत्रान् न्यपतन् अश्रुबिन्दवः । कृपया सम्परीतस्य प्रपन्नेऽर्पितया भृशम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: yasminbhagavato netrān nyapatan aśrubindavaḥ kṛpayā samparītasya prapanne
translation: The place where drops of tears fell from the eyes of the Supreme Lord who was overcome with compassion for Kardama who sought him so intensely, that is verily the Bindusara[12] surrounded by the Sarasvatī whose waters are sanctifying, pure, sweet as nectar and resorted to by multitudes of great sages.
purport:
Footnote [12]:
N.L. De identifies this near Sitpur (Siddhapura in Gujrat) about 64 miles to the north-west of Ahmedabad—GDAMI, pp. 38 and 158.

devanagari: तद्वै बिन्दुसरो नाम सरस्वत्या परिप्लुतम् । पुण्यं शिवामृतजलं महर्षिगणसेवितम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tadvai bindusaro nāma sarasvatyā pariplutam puṇyaṃ śivāmṛtajalaṃ maharṣigaṇasevitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb]

translation: The place where drops of tears fell from the eyes of the Supreme Lord who was overcome with compassion for Kardama who sought him so intensely, that is verily the Bindusara[12] surrounded by the Sarasvatī whose waters are sanctifying, pure, sweet as nectar and resorted to by multitudes of great sages.
purport:
Footnote [12]:
N.L. De identifies this near Sitpur (Siddhapura in Gujrat) about 64 miles to the north-west of Ahmedabad—GDAMI, pp. 38 and 158.

devanagari: पुण्यद्रुमलताजालैः कूजत् पुण्यमृगद्विजैः । सर्वर्तुफलपुष्पाढ्यं वनराजिश्रियान्वितम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: puṇyadrumalatājālaiḥ kūjat puṇyamṛgadvijaiḥ sarvartuphalapuṣpāḍhyaṃ vanarājiśriyānvitam

translation: It is surrounded by holy trees and mass of creepers. It is inhabited by sacred animals and sweetly singing birds. It is beautified by a charming forest rich in fruits and flowers of all seasons.

devanagari: मत्तद्विजगणैर्घुष्टं मत्तभ्रमर विभ्रमम् । मत्तबर्हिनटाटोपं आह्वयन् मत्तकोकिलम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: mattadvijagaṇairghuṣṭaṃ mattabhramara vibhramam mattabarhinaṭāṭopaṃ āhvayan mattakokilam

translation: It is vocal with warbling of crowds of joyous birds; is roamed about by (intoxicated) black-bees. It is full of noise by the dancing (and crying) of proud peacocks and the cooing of the joyful cuckoos.

devanagari: कदम्ब चम्पकाशोक करञ्ज बकुलासनैः । कुन्दमन्दारकुटजैः चूतपोतैः अलङ्कृतम् ॥ ४२ ॥

[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: It is beautified with trees such as Kadamba, Campaka, Aśoka, Karañja, Bakula, Asana, Kunda, Mandāra, Kuṭaja and young Mango trees.

devanagari: कारण्डवैः प्लवैर्हंसैः कुररैः जलकुक्कुटैः । सारसैः चक्रवाकैश्च चकोरैः वल्गु कूजितम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: kāraṇḍavaiḥ plavairhaṃsaiḥ kuraraiḥ jalakukkuṭaiḥ sārasaiḥ cakravākaiśca cakoraiḥ valgu kūjitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kūj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kūj class 1 verb]

translation: It is resounded with the sweet notes (warblings) of waterbirds like Karaṇḍava, Plava, swans, Kurara, waterfowls, cranes, ruddy-goose and Cakras.

devanagari: तथैव हरिणैः क्रोडैः श्वावित् गवय कुञ्जरैः । गोपुच्छैः हरिभिः मर्कैः नकुलैः नाभिभिर्वृतम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tathaiva hariṇaiḥ kroḍaiḥ śvāvit gavaya kuñjaraiḥ gopucchaiḥ haribhiḥ markaiḥ nakulaiḥ nābhibhirvṛtam

translation: It is visited by deer, boars, wild dogs, elephants, monkeys called Gopucchas and other species and musk deer.

devanagari: प्रविश्य तत्तीर्थवरं आदिराजः सहात्मजः । ददर्श मुनिमासीनं तस्मिन् हुतहुताशनम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: praviśya tattīrthavaraṃ ādirājaḥ sahātmajaḥ dadarśa munimāsīnaṃ tasmin hutahutāśanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The ancient king entered the sacred place along with his daughter. He saw the sage sitting after completion of the worship of fire.

devanagari: विद्योतमानं वपुषा तपसि उग्रयुजा चिरम् । नातिक्षामं भगवतः स्निग्धापाङ्गावलोकनात् । तद्व्याहृतामृतकला पीयूषश्रवणेन च ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: vidyotamānaṃ vapuṣā tapasi ugrayujā ciram nātikṣāmaṃ bhagavataḥ snigdhāpāṅgāvalokanāt tadvyāhṛtāmṛtakalā pīyūṣaśravaṇena ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ snih class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ snih class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ snih class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ snih class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ snih class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ snih class 4 verb]

translation: The sage appeared brilliant as his body had undergone austere penance, but was not ostensibly emaciated (weak) on account of the affectionate glances of the Lord at him and due to hearing the nectarlike lunar rays in the form of Viṣṇu’s words.

devanagari: प्रांशुं पद्मपलाशाक्षं जटिलं चीरवाससम् । उपसंश्रित्य मलिनं यथार्हणं असंस्कृतम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: prāṃśuṃ padmapalāśākṣaṃ jaṭilaṃ cīravāsasam upasaṃśritya malinaṃ yathārhaṇaṃ asaṃskṛtam
translation: The sage was tall. His eyes were wide like the petals of a lotus. He had matted hair. He wore bark-garments. He appeared untidy like an unpolished precious stone.

devanagari: अथोटजमुपायातं नृदेवं प्रणतं पुरः । सपर्यया पर्यगृह्णात् प्रतिनन्द्यानुरूपया ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: athoṭajamupāyātaṃ nṛdevaṃ praṇataṃ puraḥ saparyayā paryagṛhṇāt pratinandyānurūpayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ nand class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ nand ]

translation: Thereupon, the sage, being pleased with the king, who had approached his hermitage paid obeisance to him, greeted him with courteous benedictions and gave him befitting reception.

devanagari: गृहीतार्हणमासीनं संयतं प्रीणयन् मुनिः । स्मरन् भगवदादेशं इत्याह श्लक्ष्णया गिरा ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhītārhaṇamāsīnaṃ saṃyataṃ prīṇayan muniḥ smaran bhagavadādeśaṃ ityāha ślakṣṇayā girā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī ], [vocative single from √ prī ]
[nominative single from √ prī ], [vocative single from √ prī ], [accusative single from √ prī ]
[nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: The sage, remembering Lord Viṣṇu’s command spoke these pleasant words in soft and pleasant tones to the king who had accepted the reception, and took his seat modestly.

devanagari: नूनं चङ्क्रमणं देव सतां संरक्षणाय ते । वधाय चासतां यस्त्वं हरेः शक्तिर्हि पालिनी ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ caṅkramaṇaṃ deva satāṃ saṃrakṣaṇāya te vadhāya cāsatāṃ yastvaṃ hareḥ śaktirhi pālinī
translation: “Your Majesty, you are the protective power of Hari. Your tour is really for the protection of the righteous and the destruction of the wicked.

devanagari: योऽर्केन्दु अग्नीन्द्रवायूनां यमधर्मप्रचेतसाम् । रूपाणि स्थान आधत्से तस्मै शुक्लाय ते नमः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: yo rūpāṇi sthāna ādhatse tasmai śuklāya te namaḥ
translation: I bow down to you who are (the representative) of Pure Lord Viṣṇu. At appropriate time and place, you discharge the functions of the Sun, the Moon, the Fire, Indra, Vāyu, Yama, Dharma and Varuṇa.

devanagari: न यदा रथमास्थाय जैत्रं मणिगणार्पितम् । विस्फूर्जत् चण्डकोदण्डो रथेन त्रासयन् अघान् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: na yadā rathamāsthāya jaitraṃ maṇigaṇārpitam visphūrjat caṇḍakodaṇḍo rathena trāsayan aghān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ṛ ], [accusative single from √ ṛ ]
[nominative single from √ sphūrj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sphūrj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sphūrj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ tras ], [vocative single from √ tras ]

translation: When you who wield your fierce bow of terrific twang, frightening the enemies, do not go about in your victorious chariot, decked with precious stones making the whole earth quake by the trampling (march) of your army and when (if) you do not move about like the Sun leading a massive army, all the limits, i.e. rules and regulations pertaining to Varṇas and Āśramas which are laid down by Lord Viṣṇu, will be violated by the villains.

devanagari: स्वसैन्यचरणक्षुण्णं वेपयन् मण्डलं भुवः । विकर्षन् बृहतीं सेनां पर्यटसि अंशुमानिव ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: svasainyacaraṇakṣuṇṇaṃ vepayan maṇḍalaṃ bhuvaḥ vikarṣan bṛhatīṃ senāṃ paryaṭasi aṃśumāniva

[nominative single from √ kṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛṣ class 1 verb]

translation: When you who wield your fierce bow of terrific twang, frightening the enemies, do not go about in your victorious chariot, decked with precious stones making the whole earth quake by the trampling (march) of your army and when (if) you do not move about like the Sun leading a massive army, all the limits, i.e. rules and regulations pertaining to Varṇas and Āśramas which are laid down by Lord Viṣṇu, will be violated by the villains.

devanagari: तदैव सेतवः सर्वे वर्णाश्रमनिबन्धनाः । भगवद् रचिता राजन् भिद्येरन् बत दस्युभिः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: tadaiva setavaḥ sarve varṇāśramanibandhanāḥ bhagavad racitā rājan bhidyeran bata dasyubhiḥ

[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: When you who wield your fierce bow of terrific twang, frightening the enemies, do not go about in your victorious chariot, decked with precious stones making the whole earth quake by the trampling (march) of your army and when (if) you do not move about like the Sun leading a massive army, all the limits, i.e. rules and regulations pertaining to Varṇas and Āśramas which are laid down by Lord Viṣṇu, will be violated by the villains.

devanagari: अधर्मश्च समेधेत लोलुपैर्व्यङ्कुशैर्नृभिः । शयाने त्वयि लोकोऽयं दस्युग्रस्तो विनङ्क्ष्यति ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: adharmaśca samedheta lolupairvyaṅkuśairnṛbhiḥ śayāne tvayi loko

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [locative single from √ śī class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ śī class 2 verb]

translation: When you sleep (are slack), unrighteousness will be spread by men who are given to pleasures and are uncontrolled (by principles), and this world will be at the mercy of the miscreants and will meet destruction.

devanagari: अथापि पृच्छे त्वां वीर यदर्थं त्वं इहागतः । तद्वयं निर्व्यलीकेन प्रतिपद्यामहे हृदा ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpi pṛcche tvāṃ vīra yadarthaṃ tvaṃ ihāgataḥ tadvayaṃ nirvyalīkena pratipadyāmahe hṛdā
translation:
Oh Warrior, may I however ask you why you have come here? We shall sincerely be happy to comply with your wishes.”
